target.name: Stellaria
target.founder: Nicolas Breton (CEO); backed by CEA + Schneider Electric

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Stellaria is a seed-stage nuclear energy company that has raised €23M and is backed by CEA and Schneider Electric under CEO Nicolas Breton. The nuclear energy sector comprises 11 comparable companies across public and private markets: 3 public peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private competitors including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

Traditional peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable to Stellaria due to insufficient data density at the seed stage within nuclear energy. The comp universe yields zero same-stage peers, one adjacent-stage peer, and zero similar-valuation-tier matches—falling below the minimum three-datapoint threshold required for statistical reliability. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material bias given the sector-specific capital intensity and development timelines of nuclear technology.

The analysis cannot produce a triangulated valuation range without viable methodological anchors. Key uncertainties limiting valuation precision include the scarcity of seed-stage nuclear comparables, heterogeneity in reactor design approaches across the peer set, and wide variation in development stage among private peers. Further valuation work would require either (i) accessing non-public financials from one or more private peers to establish same-stage benchmarks, (ii) applying stage-adjusted revenue or cost-of-capital models, or (iii) conducting management-case scenario analysis grounded in Stellaria's technical and commercialization roadmap.
